metadata.source_paper: Satyana, A. H., & Purwaningsih, M. E. M. (2011). Collision of micro-continents with Eastern Sulawesi: records from uplifted reef terraces and proven-potential petroleum plays. Proceedings of the Indonesian Petroleum Association, 35th Annual Convention & Exhibition (IPA11-G-219).
metadata.region: East and SE Arms Sulawesi — Buton-Tukang Besi + Banggai-Sula collision
metadata.comprehension_summary: Documents evidence for two ongoing micro-continent collisions with Eastern Sulawesi: (1) Buton-Tukang Besi with SE Arm (Early Miocene to present, suture along Buton Strait), (2) Banggai-Sula with East Arm (Late Miocene to present, uplifted reef terraces as collision proxy). Both terranes are Australoid (Australian-derived). Kapantoreh ophiolites on Buton = rootless thrust slices. Tukang Besi is extension east of Buton post-collision. Banggai Basin petroleum system documented (Miocene carbonate source/reservoir). Uplift evidence: multi-level coastal terraces up to 400 m asl; ongoing collision documented by present seismicity.
main_conclusions: Buton-Tukang Besi microcontinent collided with SE Arm/Muna Block from Early Miocene; collision ongoing — Buton Strait is terrane suture
Banggai-Sula microcontinent collision with East Arm from Late Miocene; ongoing — uplifted reef terraces document progressive uplift
Tukang Besi separated from Buton as post-collision extension (not pre-collision separation)
Kapantoreh ophiolites on Buton are rootless detached thrust slices (gravity data, Milsom et al. 1999)
Both Buton-Tukang Besi and Banggai-Sula are Australoid terranes — continental Australia origin
Banggai Basin: proven sources = Miocene marls + carbonates (Matindok + Tomori Fms); reservoirs = Miocene carbonates
Buton area: proven source = Triassic Winto marine shales (type II kerogen); reservoir = Miocene carbonates

properties.name: Banggai-Sula Microcontinent — colisión brazo este
properties.full_name: Banggai-Sula Microcontinent — Australoid terrane, collision with East Arm from Late Miocene, ongoing
properties.collision_evidence: Multi-level uplifted reef terraces (up to 400 m asl in Luwuk area); active seismicity; Batui Thrust active (Hikmy et al. 2025)
properties.source_apa: Satyana & Purwaningsih (2011). IPA11-G-219, Proc. IPA 35th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Banggai-Sula collision with East Arm documented by uplifted reef terraces; Tomori PSC gas field produced in Miocene carbonates; collision ongoing
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono regional Banggai-Sula (incluyendo islas Banggai y Sula). Complementa y confirma surono_2012 y husein_2014 para este terreno.

conclusions.text: Banggai-Sula collision with East Arm Sulawesi is ongoing; multi-level uplifted reef terraces document progressive Neogene-Quaternary uplift
conclusions.geodyn_implication: Colisión activa en curso — el frente colisional Banggai-Sula define la neotectónica del brazo este y el control sobre la prospectividad hidrocarburífera (trampas de inversión Plioceno-Cuaternario)

properties.name: Buton-Tukang Besi Microcontinent — colisión brazo SE
properties.full_name: Buton-Tukang Besi Microcontinent — Australoid terrane, collision with SE Arm/Muna Block from Early Miocene
properties.tukang_besi_origin: Post-collision extensional separation from Buton (not pre-collision)
properties.kapantoreh_ophiolites: Rootless detached thrust slices on Buton (gravity data, Milsom et al. 1999)
properties.suture_zone: Buton Strait (successor basin from slight relaxation post-collision)
properties.pre_rift_stratigraphy: Permian meta-sedimentary basement; Triassic continental clastics; Jurassic-Cretaceous passive margin
properties.source_apa: Satyana & Purwaningsih (2011). IPA11-G-219, Proc. IPA 35th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Buton-Tukang Besi collision with SE Arm from Early Miocene; Buton Strait is terrane suture; Kapantoreh ophiolites = rootless thrust slices; Tukang Besi = post-collision extensional block
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono incluyendo Buton island y plataforma de Tukang Besi al este.

conclusions.text: Buton-Tukang Besi microcontinent collided with SE Arm from Early Miocene; Buton Strait = terrane suture (successor basin); Kapantoreh ophiolites are detached rootless thrust slices
conclusions.geodyn_implication: El estrecho de Buton es una cuenca de sutura post-colisional — la arquitectura extensional post-colisión separa a Tukang Besi de Buton como bloques independientes pero coetáneos

properties.name: Buton Strait — zona de sutura Buton-SE Arm
properties.full_name: Buton Strait — terrane suture zone, Buton-Tukang Besi microcontinent / SE Arm Sulawesi
properties.source_apa: Satyana & Purwaningsih (2011). IPA11-G-219, Proc. IPA 35th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Buton Strait is the terrane suture between Buton-Tukang Besi and SE Arm; interpreted as successor basin from slight relaxation of compression following collision
properties.extraction_notes: Traza corta de la zona de sutura en el estrecho de Buton. Posición geográfica bien conocida.

conclusions.text: Buton Strait = terrane suture between Buton-Tukang Besi and SE Arm; formed as successor basin from relaxation post-collision
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La sutura de Buton marca el límite tectónico entre el terreno australiano y el arco SE de Sulawesi — relevante para el análisis de provenance y los sistemas petroleros de Buton

properties.name: Banggai Basin — sistema petrolero Mioceno
properties.full_name: Banggai Basin — proven petroleum system, Miocene carbonate source/reservoir, East Arm Sulawesi
properties.proven_sources: Miocene marls + carbonates (Matindok Fm + Tomori Fm)
properties.source_apa: Satyana & Purwaningsih (2011). IPA11-G-219, Proc. IPA 35th Conv.
properties.paper_finding: Banggai Basin proven petroleum system with Miocene source+reservoir; structural traps from Banggai-Sula collision; active exploration continuing
properties.extraction_notes: Polígono aproximado de la cuenca Banggai en el área del brazo este de Sulawesi.

conclusions.text: Banggai Basin petroleum system: proven sources = Miocene marls/carbonates; trap styles range from thin-skinned thrust anticlines to wrench-related structures; active collision drives trap formation
conclusions.geodyn_implication: La colisión activa de Banggai-Sula genera trampas de HC Plio-Pleistocenas en la cuenca Banggai — sistema activo donde la tectónica y la prospectividad son coetáneas
